notes: Mackenzie's loop. The verbs are the small domestic mendings the chapter renders: pour coffee, set the wrench down, hold him, look at the garden, sit at the loom. Engine: VERB is the spendable currency (Mackenzie's quiet attention); SYNC (engine var _sync) is the positive doom-clock. The partner_at_my_pos predicate fires when Philip is at the same space. Stagnation and doubt are the negative tracks; the drip is the stagnation pulse.

cards.title: POUR COFFEE
cards.flavor: Pour from the carafe into the World's Okayest Weaver mug. Top off if half-full. Pour it down the sink and start over if the previous coffee belongs to a previous version of the morning that is now over.

cards.title: SET THE WRENCH DOWN
cards.flavor: Look at the fitting. Decide not to tighten it today. Set the wrench on the counter. The drip can win.

cards.title: HOLD HIM
cards.flavor: Cross the kitchen. Put your arms around him from behind. Let him be held for as long as he can be held. The world keeps turning around the holding.

cards.title: LOOK AT THE GARDEN
cards.flavor: Look out through the window. The tomato seedlings have grown a quarter-inch since yesterday. The bird is on the fence wire deliberating.

cards.title: SIT AT THE LOOM
cards.flavor: Sit at the bench. Pick up the threads where you left them. You did not weave for fourteen months. Then you did.

cards.title: OPEN THE ENVELOPE
cards.flavor: The boy gave it to Philip. Philip handed it to you. You turn it over. The handwriting on the front is not one you recognize.

cards.title: NAME THE BROTHER
cards.flavor: Stand in front of the wordless tapestry. Say his name out loud. You have not done this in front of the tapestry; you have done it in front of the loom, in the garden, on the couch with Philip. You have not, until today, said it where the tapestry could hear.

cards.title: SEND A TEXT
cards.flavor: Type a single word and send it. Casserole. He'll know what it means. He always has.

cards.title: STEP OUT TO THE GARDEN
cards.flavor: Through the latch that has stuck since the '21 renovation. Out into the damp earth. The salvaged driftwood pieces are under the eaves. Philip's sculpture is one of them.

cards.title: TODAY THE DRIP CAN WIN
cards.flavor: Look at the faucet. Decide it gets a day off. Tomorrow you'll fight it again. Today, no. Patience does not tick on visitors at your space for one full turn. Once per morning.
